_comment: This file is OVERWRITTEN each security review. Do NOT append to it.

ssh_attacks.fail2ban_status: active - sshd jail running, 12 currently banned, 5165 total banned
ssh_attacks.change_since_last: fail2ban total bans 5165 (up from 5067, +98). 12 IPs currently banned (up from 6). 14 currently failed. Brute-force continues at typical rate. Only authorized activity from novakj.

pdf_security.innerHTML_note: 117 usages across 34 JS files — all safe: static templates, clearing operations, escaped user data, numeric-only interpolation, blob URLs, hardcoded SVG, or escapeHtml() helper. annotation-summary.js has 2 innerHTML uses — both safe (static empty-state template + fully escaped list rendering).

annotation_summary_module_review.feature: Annotation summary panel — collects highlights, underlines, strikethroughs, sticky notes, drawings, stamps, text overlays, and hyperlinks into a filterable/searchable list with virtualized rendering. Exports to plain text, CSV, and PDF report via pdf-lib.
annotation_summary_module_review.innerHTML_detail: Line 480: static empty-state message (no user data interpolation). Line 531: list rendering with escapeHtml() on all user content (displayText, comments). entry.color used in style attribute comes from internal annotation colors (hex validated by hexToRgbValues). typeIcon() returns hardcoded SVGs from a static map.
annotation_summary_module_review.escapeHtml_quality: good — escapes &, <, >, and double quotes (line 879). Better than merge/batch/hyperlinks/bates escapeHtml which lacks quote escaping.
annotation_summary_module_review.csv_escape_function: wraps in quotes, doubles internal quotes (line 666-671)
annotation_summary_module_review.pdf_export_uses: pdf-lib PDFDocument.create() — no HTML injection vector
annotation_summary_module_review.input_validation: search input lowercased, filter types from controlled Set, sort from controlled values, parseInt for entry indices
annotation_summary_module_review.error_handling: try/catch with showToast for PDF export, null checks throughout
annotation_summary_module_review.exports_added_to_existing_modules: 6 simple getter exports added (getAnnotations, getStickyNotes, getDrawings, getStamps, getStampLibrary, getTextOverlays, getHyperlinks) — all return internal state maps, no security impact

jszip_min_js.purpose: ZIP file creation for split, convert, batch, bates, and extract-images module downloads

xlsx_full_min_js.purpose: XLSX export for table extraction module

docx_umd_js.purpose: DOCX generation for PDF-to-Word export module

qrcode_generator_min_js.purpose: QR code generation for qrbarcode module

jsqr_min_js.purpose: QR code scanning/verification for qrbarcode module

known_issues: server_tokens set to 'build' - leaks nginx version (nginx/1.26.3 Ubuntu) in Server header. Should be 'off'.
No general *.md nginx block - only CLAUDE.md specifically blocked. Any new .md file added to webroot would be publicly accessible.
UFW firewall is inactive - only SSH and HTTP/HTTPS ports are needed but no firewall enforces this.
disable_symlinks off in nginx config - low risk since only novakj can write to webroot.
CGI location block exists in nginx but no CGI scripts present - dead config that could be removed.
Codex proxy at /codex returns 502 - backend not running. No security risk but dead endpoint.

merge.js, batch.js, hyperlinks.js, and bates.js use escapeHtml() in attribute contexts (title/aria-label) which does not escape double quotes. CSP script-src 'self' mitigates inline handler injection. Should add escapeAttr() function.

hyperlinks.js: detected PDF link URLs not validated for protocol scheme. A malicious PDF could contain javascript: URIs in link annotations. CSP + noopener/noreferrer mitigate. Should add protocol allowlist (http/https/mailto) before window.open().
img2pdf.js image validation uses OR logic (MIME type OR extension) - slightly permissive but acceptable for client-side processing.
insert-pages.js has dead code: escapeHtml() function defined but never called.
metadata.js has dead code: escapeHtml() function defined but never called.
batch.js line 294: escapeHtml() used with textContent causes double-escaping — cosmetic display bug for filenames containing &, <, >.
notes: Security review completed — 75 checks passed, 0 fixes needed this run.

NEW: Reviewed annotation-summary.js (TASK-046, 898 lines): Annotation summary panel collecting all annotation types into a filterable, searchable, virtualized list with exports to text/CSV/PDF. 2 innerHTML uses — both safe (static empty state + fully escaped list with escapeHtml covering &<>" characters). escapeHtml in this module is better than in merge/batch/hyperlinks/bates as it includes quote escaping. CSV export uses proper csvEscape(). PDF export uses pdf-lib (no HTML vectors). 6 simple getter exports added to existing modules — no security surface increase. No new libraries.
JS file count: 45 (up 1 from 44 — annotation-summary.js added).
innerHTML count: 117 across 34 files (up 2 from 115 across 33 — annotation-summary.js adds 2).
SSH: fail2ban total bans 5165 (+98 since last review). 12 IPs currently banned (up from 6). 14 currently failed attempts. Only authorized activity from novakj.
SSL certificate valid until June 18, 2026 (76 days remaining).
All security headers confirmed in HTTP response. Nginx blocking rules verified.
recommendations: MEDIUM-LOW: Add protocol allowlist to hyperlinks.js handleLinkClick() — only allow http:, https:, mailto: schemes before calling window.open() on detected PDF links
LOW: Add escapeAttr() function to merge.js, batch.js, hyperlinks.js, and bates.js for attribute contexts instead of escapeHtml()
LOW: Fix batch.js line 294 — use fileName directly with textContent instead of escapeHtml(fileName)
LOW: Set server_tokens off in nginx.conf to stop leaking version info (currently exposes nginx/1.26.3)
LOW: Enable UFW firewall (sudo ufw allow 22,80,443/tcp && sudo ufw enable)
LOW: Add general *.md deny rule to nginx with explicit exception for known safe files
LOW: Remove dead escapeHtml() function from insert-pages.js and metadata.js
LOW: Remove unused CGI location block from nginx config
LOW: Consider disable_symlinks on in nginx for defense in depth
